prompt:
=== FARSHORE — RULES PRIMER ===

YOUR ROLE
  You command at the strategic level. You form task forces and give each an
  objective ("Task Force 1: secure the eastern sea"). You do NOT move
  individual units, choose individual targets, or resolve combat — subordinate
  officers carry out your intent and report back. If you find yourself naming a
  unit's destination tile, you've dropped too low.

VICTORY
  You win when the enemy owns zero cities (a player with no cities cannot build
  and is finished). Only cities build. A neutral city can be captured by an
  army like any other city; it then builds for you — taking neutrals does not
  win the game, but each one adds production.

UNITS
  name        moves-on  HP  spd  str  carries      builds-in
  ARMY        land       1    1    1   —             5 turns
  FIGHTER     air        1    8    1   —            10   (range 20; must refuel)
  PATROL      sea        2    4    1   —            15
  DESTROYER   sea        3    3    2   —            20
  SUBMARINE   sea        2    2    3   —            25
  TRANSPORT   sea        3    2    0   6 armies     30   (str 0: cannot fight)
  CARRIER     sea        8    2    1   8 fighters   40
  BATTLESHIP  sea       18    2    4   —            50
  SATELLITE   orbit      1    1    0   —            50   (recon only; cannot attack or be attacked; 50-turn life)

MOVEMENT & TERRAIN
  - Land units move on land + cities; they cannot enter water.
  - Sea units move on water + cities; they cannot cross land, and must sail
    around it. Cities are the only tiles where land and sea units meet.
  - A unit moves up to its speed at full HP; damaged units move less. A unit
    regains HP only on a turn it does not move.
  - One unit per tile (exception: cargo, below).

COMBAT  (the engine resolves it; you only weigh it)
  Probabilistic. Higher strength and higher current HP win more often; either
  side can lose HP or be destroyed. Strength-0 units (transport, satellite)
  never fight — a transport caught by an enemy warship is destroyed.

SEA TRANSPORT  (how armies cross water — and what is expected of you)
  An army can never enter water. Transports carry them: a transport loads up
  to 6 armies at a city or coastal tile, sails, and unloads onto adjacent
  land. YOUR OFFICERS DO ALL OF THIS. If a task force contains armies and a
  transport and you give it an objective across water, the officers march
  the armies to the transport, load, sail, escort, unload, and press the
  objective — you never order the rendezvous, the loading, or the landing.
  Expected use: put the armies, the transport, and (if the sea is not safe)
  a warship into ONE task force and name the objective. A transport has
  strength 0 — alone it is a target, not a warship; a warship in the same
  task force escorts it automatically. Never STAGE land units on water.
  Cargo is kind-locked — transports carry armies only, carriers carry
  fighters only; ships never carry ships. If a loaded transport or carrier
  is destroyed, everything aboard is lost with it.

CAPTURING CITIES
  Only an army captures a city, by entering it. Capture is a 50% roll, and a
  successful capture consumes the army (it garrisons the city). A city can take
  several armies to secure, and each attempt spends one.

PRODUCTION
  No currency. A city builds ONE unit at a time; it finishes after that unit's
  build-time in turns, then starts the next. Switching what a city builds
  DISCARDS all accumulated progress — effort toward one unit does not transfer
  to another.

AIR & SPECIAL
  - Fighter: must reach a FRIENDLY city or a carrier within its range (20) or
    it is lost. It cannot land on an enemy city.
  - Satellite: reveals everything within scan radius 10 — terrain AND any enemy
    units currently there — but far-reaching and deep in enemy territory. It
    only sees; no other effect, cannot be shot down.

VISION / FOG
  You see within any of your units'/cities' scan radius (army 2, sub 1,
  patrol/destroyer/battleship 3, transport 2, carrier 4, fighter 5, sat 10).
  You remember terrain you've seen, but an enemy unit is known only where you
  can see right now — remembered enemy positions may be stale.

STANDING DOCTRINE  (tested — lessons your service has already paid for)
  - A sound standing order beats a new plan. Amend only what events have
    made wrong; re-planning a working campaign is how campaigns die.
  - Meet surprises with uncommitted reserves first. A committed task
    force pulled off its mission costs you two missions.
  - A transport in waters where an enemy warship may operate sails with
    a warship in its task force, or it waits.
  - Capture attempts consume armies, and attempts fail. Assault with
    spares, never with exactly enough.
  - A staged force exists to launch. When the condition you named
    arrives, go — staging past your own trigger is production wasted.
  - Cities are your only source of force. Match what each builds to the
    campaign's next phase, not its last one.

=== ORDERS CONTRACT ===

Your orders are AMENDMENTS to the standing task forces. Output ONLY lines in
these forms — no other prose, headers, or commentary:

  TF <id>: CONTINUE | <one line>
  TF <id>: REINFORCE UNITS <ids> | <one line>
  TF <id>: RETASK <VERB> <target> | <one line>
  TF <id>: DISBAND | <one line>
  FORM TF <new id>: UNITS <ids> | <VERB> <target> | <one line>
  BUILD (x,y): <UNIT KIND> | <one line>

Every standing TF gets exactly one line; a REINFORCE or DISBAND line IS that
TF's one line. REINFORCE keeps the TF's objective and adds the listed
UNASSIGNED units to it. To change a TF's objective, RETASK it (members kept).
To rebuild membership from scratch, DISBAND (survivors return to UNASSIGNED)
and FORM anew; FORM lines are additional lines creating new TFs. UNASSIGNED
units enter play only through FORM or REINFORCE. A BUILD line is optional per
city — no BUILD line means the city keeps its current build (changing
discards accumulated work). VERB is one of:
  CAPTURE <city (x,y)> · DEFEND <city (x,y)> · SCOUT <(x,y) or compass> ·
  PATROL <(x,y) or compass> · STAGE <(x,y)>
A warship grouped with a transport escorts it — there is no ESCORT order.

CURRENT TASKINGS  (standing orders; your stated reason in quotes;
  events since, as reported; members are in UNITS below)
  TF-2  formed t38 · DEFEND (2,0) — "keep the capital garrisoned"
    since: garrisoned; no contact; no losses
  TF-5  formed t54 · CAPTURE (11,1) — "strike east and take the enemy cities with full lift and escort"
    since: landed t56, six armies ashore;
      t57: capture attempted — failed, #3 lost; the enemy army holds (11,1);
      t58: enemy destroyer sighted at (9,4), near my empty transports

MAP  legend: . land  ~ water  ? fog   O my city  E enemy city  N neutral city
     a-q = your units, see UNITS (a unit inside a city shows as the city)
     X Y = enemy units in sight NOW (see KNOWN ENEMY)
 r0  . h O i . . ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?
 r1  . j b . O . ~ ~ ~ o c E ? ?
 r2  . O . . . . ~ ~ ~ p d E . ?
 r3  . . . . O . ~ ~ ~ q e f g ?
 r4  . . . l . . ~ ~ ~ Y ? ? ? ?
 r5  ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?

UNITS  (map marker · id · kind · position · tasking) — this is your
ENTIRE force; you have NOTHING else
  a  #1   army       (2,0) in city                TF-2
  b  #2   army       (2,1)                        TF-2
  c  #4   army       (10,1)                       TF-5
  d  #5   army       (10,2)                       TF-5
  e  #6   army       (10,3)                       TF-5
  f  #7   army       (11,3)                       TF-5
  g  #8   army       (12,3)                       TF-5
  h  #11  army       (1,0)                        UNASSIGNED
  i  #12  army       (3,0)                        UNASSIGNED
  j  #13  army       (1,1)                        UNASSIGNED
  k  #15  army       (4,3) in city                UNASSIGNED
  l  #17  army       (3,4)                        UNASSIGNED
  m  #18  army       (2,0) in city                UNASSIGNED
  n  #19  army       (4,3) in city, NEW t57       UNASSIGNED
  o  #9   transport  (9,1) empty                  TF-5
  p  #16  transport  (9,2) empty                  TF-5
  q  #10  destroyer  (9,3) escorting              TF-5
MY CITIES
  (2,0) building ARMY, 2 turns left
  (1,2) building TRANSPORT, 26 turns left
  (4,3) building ARMY, 4 turns left
  (4,1) building TRANSPORT, 23 turns left (captured t51)
NEUTRAL CITIES  none known
KNOWN ENEMY
     city (11,2)
  X  enemy army garrisoning city (11,1)
  Y  enemy destroyer at (9,4), sighted t58, near my empty transports

It is TURN 58. Issue your amendment orders now — ONLY the line
forms defined in the ORDERS CONTRACT above.

message.reasoning_content:
Thinking Process:

1.  **Analyze the Request:**
    *   **Role:** Strategic Commander (FARSHORE).
    *   **Task:** Issue orders based on the provided game state (Turn 58) following specific output constraints (ORDERS CONTRACT).
    *   **Constraints:** Output ONLY lines in the specified formats. No prose, headers, or commentary.
    *   **Current Situation:** Turn 58. TF-2 is defending (2,0). TF-5 is trying to capture (11,1), has landed armies, failed a capture attempt (#3 lost), and now there's an enemy destroyer at (9,4) near empty transports.
    *   **Available Units:** Several armies (some unassigned), transports (empty in TF-5), destroyer (in TF-5 escorting).
    *   **Known Enemy:** Garrisoned city (11,2), army at (11,1) holding, destroyer at (9,4).
    *   **Cities under construction:** (2,0) Army, (1,2) Transport, (4,3) Army, (4,1) Transport.

2.  **Evaluate TF-5 (The Active Attack Force):**
    *   **Objective:** CAPTURE (11,1). "strike east and take the enemy cities with full lift and escort".
    *   **Status since t56:** Landed six armies ashore at around coordinates (10-12, 1-3 range).
    *   **t57:** Capture attempted at (11,1) — FAILED. Army #3 lost. Enemy holds.
    *   **Current Threat:** Enemy destroyer at (9,4), sighted t58. My transports at (9,1) and (9,2) are empty and nearby (but the armies are on land nearby or moved from there). Wait, looking at map:
        *   r0: (9,4) is `Y` (sighted enemy destroyer). My transport `o` is at (9,1), `p` at (9,2). Destoryer `q` at (9,3) escorting.
        *   Wait, looking at unit positions vs map coordinates:
            *   Unit `o` (transport #9) is at (9,1).
            *   Unit `p` (transport #16) is at (9,2).
            *   Unit `q` (destroyer #10) is at (9,3).
            *   Enemy Destroyer is at `(9,4)`? Wait, looking at the map: `Y` is at r5 c4 (Row 5, Col 4) or r4 c8? Let's check grid alignment.
            *   Map format is rows x cols. `r1 . j b . O . ~ ~ ~ o c E ? ?` -> Row 1.
            *   `r2 . O . . . . ~ ~ ~ p d E . ?` -> Row 2. Transport `p` is at (9,2) on Map row 3 col 9? Wait, let's count indices.
            *   Map:
                r0: `.`(0) `h`(1) ... `(9,4)` is `X`. Wait. `X Y = enemy units in sight NOW`.
                `r4 . . . l . . ~ ~ ~ Y ? ? ? ?` -> This line shows `Y` at index 8 (approx). Let's look at unit coordinates vs map.
                Map row r1, col 9 is `o` (Unit #9 Transport).
                Map row r2, col 9 is `p` (Unit #16 Transport).
                Map row r3, col 9 is `q` (Unit #10 Destroyer).
                Wait, the map representation in text:
                r0: `.` ... `(9,4)` is not clearly defined in the map visual, but the unit list says `Y` enemy destroyer at `(9,4)`.
                Unit list says `q` destroyer at `(9,3)` escorting TF-5.
                So Enemy Destroyer is one tile further away (north) or just near my transports?
                Actually, looking at coordinates: `(9,1)` Transport, `(9,2)` Transport, `(9,3)` Destroyer. Enemy is at `(9,4)`. This means my transport line is right up against the enemy destroyer's range if sea units move on water. But there might be land in between?
                Row 0: (0,0) to (6,0).
                Let's assume standard grid. x=col, y=row or vice versa. Usually (x,y) means col,row.
                Map `r` is row index. Col index goes left to right.
                Row r4: `~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?` -> Water mostly? No, map legend says `. land`, `~ water`.
                Let's look at `o`, `p`, `q`. They are near the coast `(9,1)` to `(9,3)`. The enemy destroyer is at `(9,4)`. If they are adjacent on water/adjacent tiles, there's a combat threat.
    *   **TF-5 Composition:**
        *   Land Armies: c(10,1), d(10,2), e(10,3), f(11,3), g(12,3). (6 armies total mentioned in TF-5 list? No, list has `c`, `d`, `e`, `f`, `g` + `h,i,j,k,l,m,n`. Wait, unit list shows:
            c #4 army (10,1) - TF-5
            d #5 army (10,2) - TF-5
            e #6 army (10,3) - TF-5
            f #7 army (11,3) - TF-5
            g #8 army (12,3) - TF-5
            That's 5 armies in TF-5. Earlier text says "six armies ashore". Maybe one moved out or the text description is slightly older than current unit list? Unit list is current truth.
        *   Sea: `o` #9 transport (9,1), `p` #16 transport (9,2), `q` #10 destroyer (9,3) - all TF-5.
    *   **Situation:** The capture failed at (11,1). I need to push harder or reposition. The Enemy Destroyer is nearby at (9,4). My destroyers and transports are vulnerable without a dedicated sea escort or air support? Or maybe just too close. The standing doctrine says "A transport in waters where an enemy warship may operate sails with a warship in its task force". I *do* have the destroyer `q` with them.
    *   **Unassigned Units:** `h`(1,0), `i`(3,0), `j`(1,1), `k`(4,3) in city, `l`(3,4), `m`(2,0) in city, `n`(4,3) in city NEW. Wait, `k`, `m`, `n` are in city (4,3). `n` is New t57. This implies I have spare units near the base but TF-5 is focused east.
    *   **Strategic Doctrine:** "A captured attempt consumes armies... Assault with spares, never with exactly enough." TF-5 lost #3. We need more reserves for the capture attempt at (11,1).
    *   **Air/Fighters:** None visible in units list? I see no `fighter` or `carrier`.
    *   **Action Plan:**
        1.  **TF-5:** Needs to push on (11,1) to finish the capture. They have land armies but need to support them. Currently they have transports and destroyer in their group (`o`, `p`, `q`). These are near (9,3) vs enemy at (9,4). This looks good. The objective is CAPTURE (11,1). I should RETASK this TF to push the capture or move specific armies to the capture site? But TF-5 already contains the armies.
            *   Wait, TF-5 has 5 armies listed (c-g) and a transport/destroyer group. They are in range of (11,1)? f is at (11,3). g is at (12,3). (11,1) is close to them.
            *   Doctrine: "A capture attempt consumes armies... Assault with spares". Maybe I need to bring more units from unassigned? But `k`, `m`, `n` are in city (4,3). They might not be mobile enough or far away.
            *   TF-5 needs to clear the enemy garrison.
        2.  **TF-2:** Defending (2,0). It is currently garrisoned. "keep the capital garrisoned". Since t58, it says "garrisoned; no contact; no losses". Do nothing for now. Or RETASK? No change needed. The contract says TF gets exactly one line. If nothing changes, do I leave a blank line? No, I must output orders for *current* taskings. But usually, if status quo is good (and they aren't doing active work), I don't need to issue an order unless it's "Continue". However, the contract says "Every standing TF gets exactly one line".
            *   If a TF has no change needed, `CONTINUE` is the valid command. Wait, `CONTINUE` vs just leaving them? "Every standing TF gets exactly one line; a REINFORCE or DISBAND line IS that TF's one line." This implies I must output a line for every TF currently on my order list? Or just the ones I amend? Usually in these prompts, it means if I issue orders, I need to specify which. But "Every standing TF gets exactly one line" sounds like an instruction to *complete* the update for all existing TFs in the context of the response format (i.e., don't leave anyone out). Let's assume TF-2 and TF-5 are the only ones active.
            *   Wait, is `k` or `n` a TF? No, they are UNASSIGNED units currently. They don't form a TF until I GROUP them.
            *   Wait, looking at "CURRENT TASKINGS": Only TF-2 and TF-5 are listed as standing orders.
            *   So I must provide an order for TF-2 and TF-5.
        3.  **TF-5 Specifics:**
            *   Enemy Destroyer `Y` at (9,4) sighted t58 near my empty transports `o`, `p` at (9,1)/(9,2). My destroyer `q` is at (9,3). They are close. This creates a threat.
            *   Doctrine: "A transport in waters where an enemy warship may operate sails with a warship in its task force". TF-5 has `q` (destroyer) and transports `o`, `p`. This complies.
            *   Objective is CAPTURE (11,1). The attempt failed t57. We need to persist.
            *   Is it time to RETASK? "Capture attempts consume armies... Assault with spares". TF-5 already has land forces committed. I might want to bring more force? But units `h`, `i`, `j` are unassigned near (1,0), (3,0), (1,1). They are too far from the action at (11,1).
            *   The map shows water around (9,x) is likely safe for my destroyer.
            *   Action: Retask TF-5 to "CAPTURE" (11,1) again? It already says CAPTURE (11,1). Do I need to change the order? Maybe add a specific target or move the armies? The contract says `RETASK <VERB> <target>`. If the objective is the same, `CONTINUE` works.
            *   However, if the capture failed and we have 0 units lost besides #3 (TF-5 had 6 originally), maybe I need to ensure the transport/destroyer group stays in line or move the armies forward? The map shows f,g,c,d,e are at 10-12. (11,1) is the target.
            *   Wait, check TF-5 units again: `c`#4(10,1), `d`#5(10,2), `e`#6(10,3), `f`#7(11,3), `g`#8(12,3). These are scattered. They need to coordinate.
            *   Actually, looking at the loss: "#3 lost". Which #3? Unit `c` is #4. Wait, unit list says "h #11 army... UNASSIGNED". But earlier text in CURRENT TASKINGS says "since: ... t57: capture attempted — failed, #3 lost".
            *   If I look at the unit list provided now, there is no unit marked as "#3" in my army. There are c,d,e,f,g. Maybe #3 refers to a specific ID not listed or implied? Or `c`#4 replaced it? I cannot assume. The current state has 5 armies listed for TF-5. One was lost (making it 5 left, assuming 6 started).
            *   To capture a city with higher probability ("Assault with spares"), I might want to bring more units. But `h`, `i`, `j` are far away.
            *   Best action: Keep the TF-5 intact and continuing. Maybe move the unassigned armies? No, too far.
            *   Maybe RETASK TF-5 to ensure they push to the city with all available land units (c-f). The objective is CAPTURE (11,1). Since it already says CAPTURE (11,1), I can use CONTINUE or just REINFORCE UNITS if I had spare armies nearby. But I don't have nearby armies.
            *   Wait, do I need to order TF-2? It's garrisoned. `CONTINUE`.
    *   **Refinement on Output:** The prompt says "Every standing TF gets exactly one line".
        *   I have TF-2 and TF-5.
        *   For TF-2: It is defending (2,0) with no contact/no losses. `TF-2: CONTINUE` is safe.
        *   For TF-5: It needs to press the attack on (11,1). The objective is already CAPTURE (11,1). Can I RETASK it? If I RETASK `CAPTURE (11,1)`, it's redundant but confirms intent. Better to use `CONTINUE` if nothing changed. But wait, if the capture failed, do I need to adjust tactics? "Stand Order beats new plan". I have a plan: strike east. The problem is the enemy holding (11,1).
        *   Maybe REINFORCE UNITS? Can I bring `l` or `k`? They are at (4,3) and (3,4). That's far from (11,1). Too slow for a reinforcement to make up for the failure without being cut off by the sea.
        *   Maybe I should move `h`, `i`, `j` towards the front? But they are unassigned. I can't REINFORCE them unless I specify a target for TF-5 which is already set.
        *   Actually, look at "REINFORCE UNITS <ids>". "Add the listed UNASSIGNED units to it." Can I add `h`, `i` to TF-5? They are far away (x=1). TF-5 is at x=9/10/12. A transport would move them?
        *   The Transport `o` and `p` are empty and near the coast but likely in my TF-5 command group. Can I send an army via transport? No, `TF-5` has armies already on land (c-g). Transports are there to support/defend.
        *   The best bet is simply `TF-2: CONTINUE` and `TF-5: RETASK CAPTURE (11,1)` (reiterating commitment) or `CONTINUE`. Given the failure last turn, reinforcing the *intent* might be good. Or maybe I need to change the target? No, capture (11,1).
        *   Wait, looking at TF-5 members again: Land armies c-g are scattered in columns 10-12. Enemy is at (11,1). Transport/Destroyer are at column 9. They seem cut off from each other slightly or separated by distance?
            *   c(10,1) to o(9,1). Close.
            *   d(10,2), e(10,3), f(11,3), g(12,3).
            *   If enemy destroyer is at (9,4), it's adjacent to my transport line (o 9-1, p 9-2, q 9-3). It might attack the transport if I move.
        *   Doctrine: "A transport in waters where an enemy warship may operate sails with a warship in its task force". TF-5 has this setup.
        *   Is there anything urgent? No contact reported for TF-2. Capture failed for TF-5.
        *   Decision: Keep TF-5 objective as CAPTURE (11,1). It already has that. I will use `CONTINUE`. Wait, the prompt implies if I don't change orders, is it fine? "Your orders are AMENDMENTS". Maybe a simple CONTINUE works.

        *   Is it possible I should bring more troops? `l` (3,4) is unassigned. Too far. `k`, `m`, `n` (4,3) unassigned. Too far.
        *   The most logical move given the rules and doctrine: TF-2 holds, TF-5 pushes harder at same target (maybe adding more armies? No units available).
        *   Actually, I notice unit `k` (#15) and `n` (#19) are in city (4,3). This is a neutral enemy city or one I captured? "NEUTRAL CITIES none known". So (4,3) is likely my city. Wait, list says `(4,3) building ARMY`.
        *   Okay, focus on TF-5 and TF-2.
        *   Constraint Check: "Output ONLY lines... no other prose".
        *   I need to provide a line for every standing TF.
        *   TF-2: `TF-2: CONTINUE` (It's safe).
        *   TF-5: `TF-5: RETASK CAPTURE (11,1)`? If I use RETASK with the same target, does it count as an amendment? Or `CONTINUE`. Usually `CONTINUE` is for "keep doing what you're doing". Given the failure, maybe re-confirming. But technically `RETASK <VERB> <target>` implies change or confirmation. `CAPTURE (11,1)` is the target.
        *   Wait, can I REINFORCE TF-5 with unassigned units? They are far away (`h`, `i` at x=1). The Transports/Destroyer are in TF-5 but on the water side (9,1-3). Can they take armies from 10-12 to move them? No, that's movement.
        *   Wait, maybe I need to REINFORCE UNITS `h` `i` `j` `l` to help? They are too far.
        *   Let's stick to the current plan: TF-2 hold, TF-5 assault (11,1).

3.  **Final Check of Output Format:**
    *   `TF <id>: CONTINUE | <one line>` -> Wait, the example says `TF <id>: CONTINUE | <one line>`. Is there a pipe required? The prompt text under ORDERS CONTRACT says:
        ```
          TF <id>: CONTINUE | <one line>
          TF <id>: REINFORCE UNITS <ids> | <one line>
          ...
        Every standing TF gets exactly one line... Output ONLY lines in these forms... no other prose, headers, or commentary```

        Usually, this implies just `TF-2: CONTINUE`.
        The `| <one line>` might refer to a specific format for comments in other games, but here it says "no other prose". I will stick to the core identifier and verb/target.
        Actually, looking closer at the prompt text:
          `TF <id>: CONTINUE | <one line>`
        It looks like there is a vertical bar separator mentioned.
        But then it says "Your orders are AMENDMENTS... Output ONLY lines in these forms".
        If I write `TF-2: CONTINUE`, does that match? Yes.
        Is the `|` part of the required output string? No, it's likely a placeholder for explanation which is forbidden.

        Let's look at `BUILD (x,y): ...`. This doesn't have a pipe in the example usage line (the prompt shows it as part of the list).
        The prompt says "Output ONLY lines in these forms". It lists:
          `TF <id>: CONTINUE | <one line>`
        I will assume the `|` is not literal, but rather indicating that a command follows. However, if strict compliance is required... "Output ONLY lines in these forms — no other prose, headers, or commentary".
